source_name: A Firmware Update Architecture for Internet of Things (SUIT)
requirement: Define roles for Author, Device Operator, and Trust Provisioning Authority to establish ownership of firmware update processes and trust anchor distribution.
evidence_quote: The following stakeholders are used in this document: Author... Device Operator... Trust Provisioning Authority (TPA)... The TPA distributes trust anchors and authorization policies to devices

source_name: A Firmware Update Architecture for Internet of Things (SUIT)
requirement: Implement automated, unattended firmware update mechanisms to ensure timely patching of vulnerabilities without manual intervention.
evidence_quote: Firmware updates for IoT devices are expected to work automatically, i.e., without user involvement. Automatic updates that do not require human intervention are key to a scalable solution

source_name: A Firmware Update Architecture for Internet of Things (SUIT)
requirement: Enforce authentication and integrity protection for all firmware images using manifests to prevent installation of malicious or untrusted code.
evidence_quote: The firmware image is authenticated and integrity protected. Attempts to flash a maliciously modified firmware image or an image from an unknown, untrusted source must be prevented.

source_name: A Firmware Update Architecture for Internet of Things (SUIT)
requirement: Ensure trust anchor stores resist unauthorized modification, insertion, or deletion to maintain the integrity of the cryptographic root of trust.
evidence_quote: A trust anchor store must resist modification against unauthorized insertion, deletion, and modification.

source_name: Cryptographic Inventory: Deriving Value Today, Preparing for Tomorrow
requirement: Establish a comprehensive cryptographic inventory to identify assets, assess vulnerabilities, and prioritize risk mitigation efforts for quantum readiness.
evidence_quote: Establishing comprehensive visibility of cryptographic assets within an organisation through a cryptographic inventory is essential for managing complexity.

source_name: Cryptographic Inventory: Deriving Value Today, Preparing for Tomorrow
requirement: Implement automated cryptographic discovery processes to maintain a dynamic inventory, as manual methods are insufficient for complex estates.
evidence_quote: Consequently, creating a cryptographic inventory is challenging, and automation plays a critical role in this process.

source_name: Cryptographic Inventory: Deriving Value Today, Preparing for Tomorrow
requirement: Integrate cryptographic risk management into Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) frameworks to align mitigation with business risk.
evidence_quote: Establishing a risk management framework for cryptography to integrate with ERM will be critical to ensure that mitigation efforts are prioritised.

source_name: Cryptographic Inventory: Deriving Value Today, Preparing for Tomorrow
requirement: Define accountability and responsibility for cryptographic assets to ensure proper ownership and management of the cryptographic estate.
evidence_quote: Who Is Accountable & Responsible... organisations must recognise that cryptography is now critical infrastructure – infrastructure that must be measured and managed.
evidence_location: Section 10. Who Is Accountable & Responsible / Section 2. Intended Audience

source_name: Cryptographic Inventory: Deriving Value Today, Preparing for Tomorrow
requirement: Prioritize mitigation actions based on the status of the cryptographic estate to address vulnerabilities posing significant business risks.
evidence_quote: Clarity of the status of the cryptographic estate provided by a cryptographic inventory enables the prioritisation of mitigation actions.

source_name: Related Certificates for Giving Expression to Multiple Algorithm Trust
requirement: Validate related certificate path and verify signature ownership before issuing new certificate with RelatedCertificate extension.
evidence_quote: MUST retrieve the certificate identified in the relatedCertRequest attribute... and validate it... MUST verify the signature field... SHOULD issue the new certificate containing the RelatedCertificate extension

source_name: Related Certificates for Giving Expression to Multiple Algorithm Trust
requirement: Ensure related certificate contains matching key usage and extended key usage OIDs before inclusion in the new certificate.
evidence_quote: MUST ensure that the related certificate contains the key usage (KU) bits and extended key usage (EKU) OIDs being asserted in the certificate being issued.

source_name: Related Certificates for Giving Expression to Multiple Algorithm Trust
requirement: Verify freshness of the request time and confirm the related certificate is valid at the time of issuance.
evidence_quote: MUST check that the BinaryTime indicated in the requestTime field is sufficiently fresh... SHOULD determine that the related certificate is valid at the time of issuance

source_name: Related Certificates for Giving Expression to Multiple Algorithm Trust
requirement: Restrict RelatedCertificate extension to end-entity certificates only; do not include in intermediate or root certificates.
evidence_quote: For certificate chains, this extension MUST only be included in the end-entity certificate.

source_name: Related Certificates for Giving Expression to Multiple Algorithm Trust
requirement: Do not mark the RelatedCertificate extension as critical to preserve interoperability during hybrid authentication transitions.
evidence_quote: This extension SHOULD NOT be marked critical. Marking this extension critical would severely impact interoperability.

source_name: Related Certificates for Giving Expression to Multiple Algorithm Trust
requirement: Define local policy for determining sufficient freshness of the request time in related certificate requests.
evidence_quote: Note that sufficient freshness is defined by local policy and is out of the scope of this document.

source_name: Related Certificates for Giving Expression to Multiple Algorithm Trust
requirement: Establish local policy regarding the suitability of related certificates, such as remaining validity period, before issuance.
evidence_quote: The CA may also apply local policy regarding the suitability of the related certificate, such as validity period remaining.

source_name: ML-DSA for JOSE and COSE
requirement: Validate all algorithm-related key parameters before use, including verifying the 32-byte seed length for ML-DSA private keys.
evidence_quote: When an AKP algorithm requires or encourages that a key be validated before being used, all algorithm-related key parameters MUST be validated. ... the seed length check MUST be performed.

source_name: ML-DSA for JOSE and COSE
requirement: Treat ML-DSA seeds with the same safeguards as private keys, as they can be used to compute the private key.
evidence_quote: as the seed can be used to compute the private key, it is sensitive data and shall be treated with the same safeguards as a private key

source_name: ML-DSA for JOSE and COSE
requirement: Use skEncode and skDecode algorithms when expanding the priv parameter using KeyGen_internal to ensure correct key handling.
evidence_quote: However, when the priv parameter is expanded using KeyGen_internal, the skEncode and skDecode algorithms MUST be used.
